blocks.body: Past Simple — finished actions in the past, V2 / -ed, and words like yesterday or last week.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Past Simple is used for finished actions, events, and situations in the past.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"In affirmative sentences, use V2 or the -ed form.","example":"I worked. / She visited. / They went."},{"label":"Negatives and Questions","color":"blue","text":"For negatives and questions, use did / didn't and V1.","example":"I didn't work. / Did she call?"},{"label":"Time expressions","color":"amber","text":"Words like yesterday, last night, last week, ago often point to this tense.","example":"We watched a film last night."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions. Each card includes short rules from the matching lesson.","items":[{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"V2 / -ed, regular and irregular verbs, and the main uses.","rules":[{"label":"Regular verbs","formula":"V-ed","text":"Add -ed to regular verbs.","example":"I worked. / She visited us."},{"label":"Irregular verbs","formula":"V2","text":"Use the second form for irregular verbs.","example":"They went home. / He saw Anna."},{"label":"Use","formula":"finished past","text":"Use it for finished actions, events, and short past sequences.","example":"I got up, made coffee, and left the house."}]},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"did not / didn't and the base form after did.","rules":[{"label":"All subjects","formula":"didn't + V1","text":"After didn't, the main verb returns to the base form.","example":"I didn't work. / She didn't call."},{"label":"Full form","formula":"did not + V1","text":"The full form sounds more formal or stronger.","example":"They did not visit us."}]},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Did you...? / Did he...? and short answers.","rules":[{"label":"Question","formula":"Did + subject + V1?","text":"Put Did at the beginning and keep the main verb in V1.","example":"Did you work yesterday? / Did she call?"},{"label":"Short answers","formula":"did / didn't","text":"Repeat did or didn't in short answers.","example":"Yes, I did. / No, she didn't."}]},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"yesterday, last night, ago, in 2022, and other past markers.","rules":[{"label":"Finished time","formula":"yesterday / last...","text":"These markers show a finished time in the past.","example":"We watched a film last night."},{"label":"Distance from now","formula":"ago","text":"Ago shows how much time has passed since something happened.","example":"I saw him two days ago."},{"label":"Past period","formula":"in + year","text":"A specific past year or finished period also points to Past Simple.","example":"They moved here in 2021."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Finished action","color":"emerald","description":"Use it for something that happened and is complete.","examples":[{"en":"I finished my homework yesterday.","ua":"The action is complete."},{"en":"She called me in the evening.","ua":"A finished event in the past."}]},{"label":"Past time or period","color":"blue","description":"Use Past Simple when the time is finished: yesterday, last week, in 2021.","examples":[{"en":"We lived in Gdansk in 2021.","ua":"A finished past period."},{"en":"They visited us last weekend.","ua":"A finished weekend in the past."}]},{"label":"Short story","color":"amber","description":"The tense works well when you tell several past actions one after another.","examples":[{"en":"I got up, made coffee, and left the house.","ua":"A short sequence of past actions."},{"en":"He opened the door and smiled.","ua":"One event followed another."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Past Simple is used for finished actions and events in the past.","In affirmative sentences, use V2 or the -ed form.","For negatives and questions, use did / didn't and V1.","Words like yesterday, last..., and ago often point to this tense."]}
